reviews.review_description:
This has become one of my go to games when I want a workout. The movement controls take a little getting used to, but then it really feels like you're running/racing. I work up a sweat but don't even realize it until after the track is completed because I'm so immersed in it. 

I would love more tracks, but there's a good variety already, and I'll definitely be replaying them multiple times. If they could make this game multiplayer, that would be epic. Imagine a racing game where you actually have to put effort in to win! Even if it was, like, an adjacent track, or a shadow racer (of friends, or of my own previous runs) that would motivate me to push myself even more.

I hate exercising but this 
..this is great...I love it ..the free trial got me & I immediately purchased

I would give this game 5 stars if I didn't have to hold down the grip button, or if there was an option to not have to hold it. I don't understand why swinging arms couldn't be enough and I struggle holding grip down for that long, so it's an accessibility issue.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words and helpful feedback! 🙏 We totally get that holding the grip can feel like a bit much for some players. Right now, it’s designed to help you stop moving on command and avoid accidental motion detection—but we absolutely hear your concerns. The good news: we’re already exploring improvements! One idea is to add an option so players don’t need to hold the grip at all. We’re testing things out and will keep working to make Sportvida CyberDash smoother and more accessible for everyone 💪 Really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts—please keep them coming via socials or email anytime!

🔄Update:
Good news — we’ve just released the new “Swing-Only Running” mode! 🎉
Now you can run just by swinging your arms — no more holding the grip button!
You can switch it on or off anytime in the settings.
Hope this makes your workout feel more comfortable!

reviews.review_title: MY best VR workout so far
reviews.review_description:
I go all-out in *Thrill of the Fight*, but I lose control and burn out within two minutes (I should probably be too embarrassed to admit that). *Sportvida*, on the othe
r hand, keeps my exertion sustainable without hitting the wall. I hesitate to say it beats *Les Mills BodyCombat* and that other subscription based offering... but honestly, *Sportvida* outshines them all.

I was hooked after the free trial.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your comment! 🙌
We’re really happy that Sportvida helped you keep going without burning out — that’s exactly the kind of experience we were aiming for! Hearing that it stands out even more than some big titles means a lot to our team.
Thanks again for supporting us since the trial — we’ll keep working hard to make workouts even more fun and energizing! 💪🧡

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: When I first played Sportvida, I provided some suggestions to the dev team. Looks like they delivered! They added new modes to include race mode, which is just the tracks with no obstacles!! So excited to see this! Thanks, devs!!

I very much enjoyed Sportvida. The tutorial was thorough and helpful. Take your time with the tutorial and practice mode on easy. I was able to grasp the movement quite quickly and pleasantly surprised how awesome it felt to skate. I didn’t even experience any motion sickness but it takes rollercoasters in VR to make me sick (intense comfort). The comfort rating for Sportvida is “moderate” to compare. Check out my YT video @misschiefvr for demo gameplay.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your support and encouragement! We’re absolutely thrilled you noticed the new modes and enjoyed them so much! 🎉
Also, big thanks for sharing your suggestions and gameplay experience—it really helps other players understand what our game is all about!

Our team watched your video and loved it!
With more stages and new mode combinations coming up, things will only get more exciting. We can’t wait to see you keep playing—and to catch more awesome gameplay on your channel! 💪

reviews.review_description: I enjoy the sensation of speed you get in this game. I really currently have only one issue and that is the need to continously press down buttons to run. If running is continous holding down buttons while swinging your arms seems superflous.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for enjoying our game, and we truly appreciate your feedback!

There has been some discussion within our team regarding the running mechanics. Some prefer a more intuitive running experience, while others believe that maintaining control is important for new players. To strike a balance between these perspectives, we recently updated the button mechanics to make running more comfortable while keeping the game’s rhythm intact.

We recommend giving the latest update a try to see if it feels better for you! If you have any further thoughts, we’d love to hear your feedback. 🎮😊

reviews.review_description:
**My Mini Guide for an Easier Start**

Sportvida – An Intense VR Fitness Game with a Steep Learning Curve 
Sportvida is a unique VR workout game that takes you on a neon-lit racetrack in space. However, the controls require skill, and motion sickness can be a significant challenge for some players.  

The game tracks your movement direction through your gaze, but I recommend adding real-world motions, such as leaning and looking, to enhance the natural connection between your physical actions and the virtual environment. This approach makes the controls more intuitive for your brain to process.

# First Stage: Mastering Movement Mechanics

1. Focus on Basics First:
   Start with the tutorial, which introduces the core movement mechanics. Stick to basic movement before tackling targets.  

2. Practice in the Main Menu:
   In the main menu, you’ll find three campaigns on the left: *“Punch,” “Slash,”* and *“Ram.”* On the right, select the "Easy" practice mode. You can choose between tracks or tracks with targets. Once you’re confident, turn off practice mode to compete on the leaderboards.

---
*Tips for Reducing Motion Sickness*
 
- Take Small Steps: 
  Begin with short sessions and gradually increase your playtime. Your body needs time to adjust to the new movement mechanics.  
- Sensory Focus: 
  - Eat something with a strong flavor, like a piece of orange or citrus candy, to shift focus from nausea. Be cautious not to choke while playing.  
  - A drop of citrus oil on a cotton pad nearby can help through scent.  
- Ventilation Matters:  
  Use a fan to blow cool air on your face, simulating a sense of real-world motion and reducing dizziness.  
- Start Simple: 
  Practice on straight tracks first, and tackle curves or loops only once you feel more confident.


*Tips for Balance and Coordination*
 
- Practice Sitting or Kneeling:
   If standing feels overwhelming, start by practicing arm movements and leaning while seated or kneeling. This reduces balance-related challenges.  
- Use a Small Carpet as Orientation Aid:  
   A round carpet or mat can help you stay in place and avoid unintentional movements.  
- Controlled Movements Over Speed: 
   Avoid rushing. Smooth, deliberate movements improve your balance and help you master the mechanics faster.  

—
# Second Stage: Campaign Mode
  
Once you’ve chosen a campaign mode, focus on both speed and gameplay elements like smashing targets or mastering squats while making use of practice mode.  Each completed level opens up new ones, continuing your fitness journey.
Remember: Nobody expects perfection. Progress comes with practice, and every little improvement is a victory.  

Sportvida is not for the impatient, but perseverance rewards you with a truly immersive fitness experience. Whether you’re new to VR or a seasoned player, every bit of progress matters. With the right mindset, you’ll unlock the full potential of this game.  

---
*Why I Wrote This Review* 
I decided to rewrite my first review to help new players overcome movement challenges.  

I purchased the game myself and have no connection to the developers – just a regular VR fitness enthusiast who loves trying out new fitness games. Over several days, I completed all available levels in the early access version (1.5.40, January 2025).  

I genuinely enjoyed the variety Sportvida offers. My usual fitness routine spans a large library of rhythm-based games, from note-cutting and rhythmic boxing to target punching and wall-dodging – even dancing! Sportvida integrates many of these concepts into different racing modes in a fresh, challenging way.  

Unfortunately, based on the limited leaderboard activity and negative reviews, it’s clear this great game is struggling to gain traction. It would be a shame to see such potential go unrealized.  

Remember, be kind to yourself, have fun, and enjoy the journey to a fitter you!


——
Original first review:

Sportvida is an intense VR workout game where players race along a floating, neon-lit track in space, competing against time. Its retro cyber aesthetic with glowing elements adds to the futuristic vibe. Currently, the game supports only English and Chinese languages and offers solo play.

Players run by swinging their arms to gain speed while navigating curves through sideways leaning and looking into the direction or physically turning for sharper angles. Smashing targets, dodging obstacles, and performing squats or other motions seems to contribute to the final score. The game also includes modes like “slash”, “boxing target” , or “ram” though I haven’t explored all of them yet.
While the tutorial introduces the basics, it lacks depth, leaving some mechanics unexplained and requiring trial-and-error learning during gameplay.

This game is challenging and not ideal for VR beginners. Rapid movements and constant turning may cause motion sickness, particularly for those new to intense VR experiences. Physical exertion also limits playtime.
If you’re comfortable with Synth Riders in spiral mode: wild, where directional control relies on shifting balance, you may find adapting to Sportvida easier. However, mastering the mechanics still demands effort and endurance.

Currently in early access (Version 1.5 - Jan 2025), not all modes and levels are available yet. Nonetheless, the existing content is promising, and I’m optimistic about its potential as a fitness game.
For those seeking an engaging way to get fit and tackle high-intensity challenges, Sportvida is worth trying. Its mix of racing, obstacle-dodging, and target smashing, it offers a fun full-body workout at your own pace, free from multiplayer stress.
Personal Tip: You don’t need a large play area. A small round carpet can help maintain orientation and prevent accidents during physical turns.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write such a detailed and thoughtful review!

We’re truly delighted to see your support and enthusiasm for Sportvida CyberDash. Not only did you share your passion for the game, but you also provided so many practical tips and insights that will undoubtedly be incredibly helpful for other players!

We especially appreciate the beginner’s guide and tips on overcoming motion sickness that you included. These align perfectly with our goal of helping players enjoy intense workout challenges while discovering the fun and excitement of the game.

Your support and feedback mean the world to us, and it inspires us to keep improving Sportvida CyberDash to make the experience even better in the future! Once again, thank you for writing such an insightful and comprehensive review. We hope you continue to enjoy the challenges and fun that Sportvida CyberDash has to offer!

Wishing you a fantastic day!
— ible Sportvida Team

reviews.review_description:
I did not quite get behind the physics of movement direction.
Its hard for me to get used to steering with my headsets direction, since we humans can walk in a direction while looking not straight forward.
I have the guess that often my eyes move instead of my head which makes steering more difficult.
I wonder if a crosshair could help by indicating the movement direction while enabling my eyes to look ahead on the track.
Another option would be a colored trajectory on the floor that shows the predicted path.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for supporting our game and taking the time to share your thoughts!

We greatly value the feedback you provided regarding the controls and would love to gain a clearer understanding of your experience. It appears that we received a related inquiry from you on our website, and we have already sent a reply via email. We look forward to hearing back from you soon.

If you have not received our email, please feel free to reach out to us directly through our community platforms. We’re excited to hear more of your thoughts and are committed to providing you with the best possible gaming experience!

Thank you again for your support, and we hope you enjoy the game!
— ible Sportvida Team

reviews.review_description:
Disclaimer: I got this game for free, but I will try my best to give an unbiased opinion.

The game is really fun, I did all the tutorials and the 4 first courses on RAM mode (which is my favorite) and I was already ready for a shower, this game makes you sweat if you tale it seriously.

The tutorial part lets you test some of the modes, which at first I thought they will combine all at once during Gameplay, and I was thinking through that it was going to be too difficult or convoluted Gameplay but it is not, they fail to make it clear that these are separate modes, and the tutorial is well made, but it feels a little long some times 

My favorite Mode is RAM Mode, I feel like this should be the Main Mode, because it lets you concentrate on the skating, dodging and squatting part of the Gameplay, for me it was not natural to have to hit targets with my hands while skating in the others modes, the idea is like It could be fun, but in practice wasn't for me, I prefer to blast through panels with my body, and actually is the more thought out Mode, because if you don't stand fully after a squat obstacle, you can't blast the complete panel and it doesn't count as full points, it's a clever system imho for this kind of purpose.

I have to see yet the more difficult courses, but what I was inmmediately thinking is that It would be even more fun, if there was some sections in which go down a slope at full velocity for a few seconds, then normalize and then another slope and go on, more verticality, or even sections with jumps and ramps, but maybe they are there in more advanced tracks.

Maybe you could add some extra optional points scattered through some difficult turns that you can only grab if you skate near them, or maybe a slight drift mechanic.

And I was thinking that the movement system feels so right that you can actually made a Rollerball or Hyperblade  multiplayer Mode, It would be really fun.

My reason to not give It 5 stars right away is that I feel that you can still build upon this concept and mechanics, and I feel like I want to see It even more fleshed out, but if I progress more through the game and these things are there, I will modify the review.

Keep the good work

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed review and suggestions! 😊 We’re thrilled to hear you enjoy the game, especially the Ramming mode! This mode truly allows everyone to focus on sprinting and dodging. 💪 Your suggestions about adding slopes, jumps, drift mechanics, and multiplayer modes are incredibly helpful, and we will seriously consider these ideas! While we may not be able to implement these suggestions right away, we’ll definitely keep them in mind for future updates as we strive to enhance the gaming experience.

We’ll also reflect on the design of the tutorial mode to ensure it provides clearer guidance for players without impacting the overall experience. The upcoming levels are packed with diverse designs and challenges just waiting for you to discover! Keep up the enthusiasm for exercise and continue to challenge yourself! Our team is committed to making the game even better, and we hope you’ll continue to support us! 🔥

reviews.review_description:
I’ve only ran twice. First impression is it’s hard to move forward in the normal setting. I swing my hands fast but I was barely moving. 

Had to change the settings to easy to move. 

I find it bothersome to hit the target while running and doing turns carefully so I don’t fall down from the path. 

Also, it’s a little disorienting and causes a bit of motion sickness. I wish it’s on a rail so I can just focus on swinging my arms.

The graphics for the targets are passable but could have been higher rez.

I haven’t tried other modes like the slashing as I was feeling motion sickness

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts with us. We've received several comments about the energy buildup during the initial running phase. As you noted, our team originally designed it to provide an effective workout, but it seems we didn't quite get the balance right.
We've listened to the feedback and have released an update to make running easier. This week, we're also adding more tutorial sections to help with the starting actions.
Your suggestions are valuable to us, and we'll share them with our team for future improvements. We hope you'll have a more enjoyable experience the next time you play.
We appreciate your patience and support as we work to enhance the game. Thank you for being part of our community.

reviews.review_description: I ended up getting this game for free so here's my review. It has fun levels which make you work to beat your previous times and honestly it's a really fun. Not only is it fun, but it also gets in a good workout. I would say my hopes for the future would be mod support but we'll have to see. The best thing about it unlike other fitness games is that it's a one and done deal. Once you buy the game, there isn't a subscription or anything.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. Your support motivates our team to keep developing.

reviews.review_description:
It’s a really well made fitness/sport game.👍The gameplay is absolutely great and intense, movement system blast.🔥 I had absolutely zero issues so far! But please increase the default render resolution, so people won’t need to use third party methods for that.
Great job!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your support and kind words! 😊 We're really happy to hear you're enjoying the intense gameplay and smooth movement system. 🔥 We'll definitely take your suggestion about the render resolution to heart and work to make the experience even better for everyone. We can’t wait for you to keep dashing and enjoying the fun! 💪

reviews.review_description:
Overall, this game for me is at the "so much potential" stage. It's quite fun and, while it looks a bit like Beat Saber, its gameplay feels very unique. This is fun but first and foremost is a proper workout. After playing for about ten minutes, my watch began pinging me to record an indoor run (which it's never done before) if that's any indication of activity level. 

There's a tutorial but unfortunately it isn't interactive. I think allowing a small space/loop to practice the motion would be helpful.

Unlike other rhythm games where blocks, drums, or whatever flies at you, in CyberDash you pump your arms to gain momentum and move yourself forward. This led my first frustration. Sometimes I'd just stop. No idea why, because I was still holding down the buttons and my arms were moving like crazy but I'd just stand still. Then, without anything changing in my movements, I'd suddenly rocket forward. This is most noticeable at the start of the level where I usually swing my arms for 3-5 seconds before there's any sign of movement, but it happened a couple times during levels too. I'd notice the momentum bars falling, but my arm movements weren't filling them up. Then suddenly my movements would start counting and the bar would fill up within 1-2 seconds and I was flying again.

The other frustration was on punching multiple blocks at the same time. With one block, my streaks were almost perfect. With two blocks, suddenly it's like I can't hit the side of a barn. I'd usually get one of the two blocks but very rarely two and occasionally neither. You can see your "hands" with fluorescent bands around them and I noticed that even when I extended both arms outwards into the blocks, I'd usually only see one of my hands.

I'm going to keep trying it out because this was a fun way to get my heart rate up, but until either I can figure out why I'm not punching multiple blocks or tracking is fixed (don't really know which one), it's not going to be a daily driver.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed suggestions; they are immensely helpful! We apologize for any frustration during gameplay, but please don't give up on our game. Our team is continuously making adjustments based on player feedback, and we will notify you as soon as updates are available.

Regarding your issues:
Movement Issues:
1.	An interactive tutorial is in progress and will be included in the next major update.
2.	If you don't mind continuing to play without the interactive tutorial, we recommend trying the following:
•	Watching our YouTube tutorials for gameplay tips: (https://www.youtube.com/@Sportvida-CyberDash)
•	Using Running Practice Mode or Easy Mode to get a feel for the movements. Starting is like riding a bicycle – the beginning is the hardest, but it gets easier once you get going.
•	If you notice any irregularities in the controls, try using the calibration feature to correct any unknown deviations.
Target Hitting Issues:
We understand you have issues with hitting targets but need more details to fully grasp the problem. If it's convenient for you, could you join our Discord channel (https://discord.gg/hGwyMV5jkZ) to discuss the details with us?
Possible issues might be:
1.	Sprint speed is too fast, leading to a short reaction time. We have a speed adjustment feature in testing and will be available after testing is complete.
2.	Difficulty seeing where you are aiming. Improvements are planned, but need time to design and implement.
If these aren't the issues, please provide more details to help us improve the game.

Thank you again for your thoughtful suggestions, and we apologize for any frustration caused. We're still working hard, so please don't give up on CyberDash. Thank you!

🔄Update:
1.	We have now launched an interactive tutorial to help players get started more easily! Please give it a try and let us know how your experience goes!
2.	Due to internal community adjustments, our Discord channel will be temporarily closed. If you have any questions or suggestions, please reach out to us on Twitter (https://twitter.com/Sportvida__), where we’ll continue providing support.

reviews.review_description: I bought this game, there is a big difficulty that I did not expect, can you allow the location to be fixed so that I do not fall while running?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your support! We have been continuously optimizing the game based on player feedback to ensure a more enjoyable experience. Recently, we have made some adjustments to the running difficulty and interactive tutorials, which were inspired by valuable player suggestions. To better understand your needs, we would love to hear your thoughts. We would like to know if you have the following expectations:

◾Would you prefer to only control forward movement, without having to control left or right direction? (For example, by swinging your arms to control speed, allowing the character to automatically follow the track and turn.)
◾Would you prefer to avoid the sensation of falling? (For example, when you go off the track, the screen would go black, reset you back on the track, and then reopen the screen, without the intermediate feeling of falling.)
◾Would you like to play on a straight track, avoiding the need for turning control? (For example, running in a straight line while hitting targets.)
If you are willing to share more suggestions and would like to discuss further, feel free to reach out to us via Twitter(https://x.com/Sportvida__) or our Facebook fan page(https://www.facebook.com/SportvidaCyberDash/)! We look forward to hearing your valuable feedback!
